Emancipation, deliverance, freedom, liberation, escape, release; — release from worldly existence, future emancipation, final beatitude (resulting from the soul's final liberation and its exemption from further transmigration); — the act of loosing, loosening, untying, unbinding, undoing, solving, &c.; — acquittance of an obligation

s.m. (dialec.) = mothrā, q.v. s.m. (lit. 'a lump'), A swelling in one of the joints (of a horse), spavin; a splint.

def: part. adj. Secluding oneself in a mosque or temple for religious service; continually at prayer in the temple; constant in devotion; — attentive to religious duties.
s.m. Weeping, lamentation.

ety: prob. akin to S. मोच, caus. of rt. मुच्
def: s.m. A stocking; — a boot; — a glove
subs: adj. & s.m. Biting (or a horse that bites) at the foot or leg of his rider.

ety: pass. part. of اعظم, iv of (and syn. with
def: s.m. The superior, the greater, the principal, chief; the greater part; the better sort; the gross, or mass, or bulk (of).
wety: Borrowed from Arabic مُعَظَّم (muʕaẓẓam).

def: adj. = مردو mr̤idu, q.v.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مرد (mard).

def: s.m. & (in Urdū) f. A kind of drum (broader at the middle than at the ends), a double drum, a tabour (syn. pakhāwaj); — (in Geom.) a figure shaped like the mr̤idang or drum; — a kind of (glass) shade (for a light or lamp); — a noise, sound; — a bamboo.
adj. Soft, tender; mild, gentle; affable, kind; quiet; — weak; moderate; slow; tame; blunt, dull, lacking sharpness
subs: s.m. Gentle conversation, quiet talk; pleasing intercourse
adj. Of a mild or gentle disposition; naturally soft, or easy, or tame
vulg. mr̤idu-bāṅī, s.f. Soft or mild of speech, sweet-voiced (syn. shīrīn-zabān).

def: adj. Soft, mild, tender, gentle; — s.m. Water
subs: adj. Of a gentle or tender nature; tender-natured.
